mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a combined Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns during recovery phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic activity, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 4.0% year-over-year, central banks tighten policy and real discount rates rise, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in total nonfarm payrolls and a rise in the unemployment rate confirms a deteriorating labor market, reducing consumer spending capacity and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45% during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases do not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
